prompt: Draft a detailed cryo-EM sample preparation protocol for the influenza A H3N2 hemagglutinin (HA) ectodomain trimer for high-resolution structure determination of antibody-bound complexes. The protocol should include: (1) protein preparation: HA0 ectodomain with furin site mutation (R329Q) to prevent cleavage, C-terminal T4 foldon + His8 tag, expressed in HEK293F cells at 1–2 mg/mL in 20 mM HEPES pH 7.4, 150 mM NaCl, (2) antibody Fab complex preparation: mix HA trimer with Fab (1.5× molar excess per monomer), 30 min on ice, purify by SEC on Superdex 200 Increase 10/300, (3) cryo-EM grid preparation: plasma-clean Quantifoil R1.2/1.3 Cu 300 mesh grids, apply 3 µL sample at OD280 ~0.5, blot 4 s at 4°C 100% humidity, plunge into liquid ethane (Vitrobot Mark IV), (4) screening: check ice thickness and particle distribution on Glacios 200 kV, (5) data collection: 300 kV Titan Krios with Falcon 4 detector, target 1.5–2.5 Å, (6) particle picking strategy and 2D class selection criteria. Include notes on preferred antibody orientations for epitope mapping.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and cryo-EM methodology literature; target: influenza A H3N2 HA; organism: Influenza A virus; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_name: Influenza hemagglutinin trimer cryo-EM sample preparation
_provenance.target_class: orthomyxovirus surface glycoprotein cryo-EM structural determination
